{{ill|Habikino|qid=Q694308|1=Habikino}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.56277778|N|135.60916667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|part_of={{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|qid=Q55524104|1=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group}}; {{ill|Aoyama Kofun|qid=Q66124385|1=Aoyama Kofun}}; {{ill|Bokeyama Kofun|qid=Q11338602|1=Bokeyama Kofun}}; {{ill|Futatsuzuka Kofun|qid=Q11371232|1=Futatsuzuka Kofun}}; {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|qid=Q11429120|1=Hakayama Kofun}}; {{ill|Hazamiyama Kofun|qid=Q11275423|1=Hazamiyama Kofun}}; {{ill|Higashiumazuka Kofun|qid=Q66124380|1=Higashiumazuka Kofun}}; {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|qid=Q11479972|1=Ichinoyama Kofun}}; {{ill|Jōganjiyama Kofun|qid=Q66124384|1=Jōganjiyama Kofun}}; {{ill|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun|qid=Q11637613|1=Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun}}; {{ill|Komuroyama Kofun|qid=Q11411025|1=Komuroyama Kofun}}; {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|qid=Q11631432|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}}; {{ill|Konda-Maruyama Kofun|qid=Q11631425|1=Konda-Maruyama Kofun}}; {{ill|Kurizuka Kofun|qid=Q66124381|1=Kurizuka Kofun}}; {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|qid=Q11476069|1=Minegazuka Kofun}}; {{ill|Mitsuzuka Kofun|qid=Q11354002|1=Mitsuzuka Kofun}}; {{ill|Mukōhakayama Kofun|qid=Q11415923|1=Mukōhakayama Kofun}}; {{ill|Nabezuka Kofun|qid=Q11650613|1=Nabezuka Kofun}}; {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|qid=Q11378467|1=Nakatsuyama Kofun}}; {{ill|Nishiumazuka Kofun|qid=Q66124383|1=Nishiumazuka Kofun}}; {{ill|Nonaka Kofun|qid=Q11645001|1=Nonaka Kofun}}; {{ill|Oka Misanzai Kofun|qid=Q11471921|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}}; {{ill|Q11418587|qid=Q11418587|1=Karatoyama Kofun}}; {{ill|Q11645006|qid=Q11645006|1=Nonaka Miyama Kofun}}; {{ill|Shimaizumi Maruyama Kofun|qid=Q11476778|1=Shimaizumi Maruyama Kofun}}; {{ill|Shiragayama Kofun|qid=Q11580492|1=Shiragiyama Kofun}}; {{ill|Takaya Hachiman-yama Kofun|qid=Q11669270|1=Takaya Hachiman-yama Kofun}}; {{ill|Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun|qid=Q11669285|1=Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun}}; {{ill|Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun|qid=Q11555978|1=Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun}}; {{ill|Ōtorizuka Kofun|qid=Q11441985|1=Ōtorizuka Kofun}}|Historic Site={{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}; {{ill|part of UNESCO World Heritage Site|qid=Q43113623|1=part of UNESCO World Heritage Site}}}}
{{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox Cemetery |name = Furuichi kofungun |image = Kondagobyoyama Kofun air.jpg |imagesize = 250px |caption = Kondagobyōyama Kofun. This is the largest in the group. |map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture |map_size = 250 |established = |location = [[Habikino]], [[:en:Fujiidera, Osaka|Fujiidera]], {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} |coordinates = {{coord|34|33|46|N|135|36|33|E|region:JP_dim:2000|display=inline,title}} |type = |owner = |size = |graves = |website = |findagrave = |political = | embedded = {{Designation list | embed=yes | designation1 = WHS | designation1_offname = [[Mozu tombs|Mozu]]-Furuichi [[Kofun]] Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan | designation1_date = [[List of World Heritage Sites by year of inscription#2019 (43rd session)|2019]] <small>(43rd [[World Heritage Committee|session]])</small> | designation1_number = [https://whc.unesco.org/en/list/1593/ 1593] | designation1_criteria = {{UNESCO WHS type|(iii), (iv)}}(iii), (iv) | designation1_free1name = Region | designation1_free1value = [[List of World Heritage Sites in Eastern Asia|Eastern Asia]] }}{{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} .This is the second largest in the group.]]
{{Nihongo|'''Furuichi kofungun'''|古市古墳群}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofuns]] located in the cities of [[:en:Fujiidera, Osaka|Fujiidera]] and [[:en:Habikino, Osaka|Habikino]], {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.city.fujiidera.osaka.jp/9,1053,97,141.html |title=古市古墳群ってなに？ |publisher=[[:en:Fujiidera, Osaka|Fujiidera City]] |accessdate=8 May 2011 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719143924/http://www.city.fujiidera.osaka.jp/9,1053,97,141.html |archivedate=19 July 2011 }}</ref> Twelve of the tumuli in this group were individually designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956, with an additional 14 collectively added to the designation in 2001, and the area under protection expanded in 2018.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1798 |title=古市古墳群|trans-title=Furuichi kofungun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref><ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==Overview== The Furuichi Kofun Cluster extends over an area of 2.5 kilometers north-to-south by four kilometers east-to-west, covering plateaus and hill with an average elevation of 24 meters above sea level. These tumuli were built between the late 4th and the mid-sixth century AD. Twenty-seven, including many of the larger tumuli, are under the control of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} and are classified as "imperial tombs", for which [[archaeological excavation]] has been prohibited.
In 2010 the Furuichi kofungun cluster of tumuli, along with those of [[:en:Mozu kofungun|Mozu kofungun]], were proposed for inscription on the {{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|qid=Q7809|lt=UNESCO|1=UNESCO}} [[:en:World Heritage List|World Heritage List]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/tentativelists/5570/ |title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofungun, Ancient Kofun Clusters |publisher={{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|qid=Q7809|lt=UNESCO|1=UNESCO}} |accessdate=8 May 2011}}</ref> On 6 July 2019, [[Mozu tombs|Mozu]]-Furuichi [[Kofun]] Group was inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site under Criteria: (iii), (iv).<ref>{{cite web|url=https://en.unesco.org/news/seven-cultural-sites-inscribed-unescos-world-heritage-list|title=Seven cultural sites inscribed on UNESCO's World Heritage List|website=UNESCO|date=6 July 2019}}</ref>
== Decline of The Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group == After the {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|en|Ichinoyama Kofun|qid=Q11479972|lt=Ichinoyama Kofun|1=Ichinoyama Kofun}}, the era of grand tomb groups began to decline. Though a few large tombs were still erected, like the {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|qq=}}, the frequency and size began to diminish. The Kawachi Otsukayama Kofun is an outlier, situated between the [[Mozu Tombs|Mozu]] and Furuichi Kofun Groups, was built, measuring a staggering 355 meters in length. Its construction during this period remains a mystery, calling for further research and analysis.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |title=Explanation: the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group(Part 2)│Nonaka Kofun/Osaka University Department of Archaeology |url=https://www.let.osaka-u.ac.jp/kouko/nonaka3d2023/en/explanation_part_02.html |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=野中古墳／大阪大学考古学研究室 |language=ja-JP}}</ref>
As the Furuichi Kofun Group progressed past its prime, the center of tomb construction transitioned to locations such as Shinagadani (also referred to as Kawachi-Asuka), near present-day Taishi Town. The primary locus for tombs in the Kawachi region shifted from Mozu and Furuichi to areas like Shinaga.<ref name=":1" />
== Types == *{{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} ("keyhole-shaped) : formerly 31, 26 survive *{{nihongo|''[[Round Kofun|empun]]''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ("circular"): formerly 30, 5 survive *{{nihongo|''[[Square Kofun|hōfun]]''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} ("square"): formerly 48, 22 survive *indeterminate: formerly 14, 34 survive
Total: formerly 123, 87 survive
=== Gallery === <gallery> File:Kondagobyoyama Kofun air.jpg|Kondagobyoyama Kofun File:墓山古墳と陪塚.jpg|Hakayama Kofun File:Oka Misanzai Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|{{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|qq=}}|alt= File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Karusato Otsuka Kofun </gallery>

Kofungun List (Furuichi)]（Mozu-Furuichi Kofungun World Cultural Heritage Registration Promotion Headquarters Meeting）</ref> !Name !Name !Type !Length !National Historic Site !Imperial Household Agency !World Heritage Site & Comments |- |{{ill|Kondayama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}|| Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|qq=}}|| 岡ミサンザイ古墳||keyhole||242m|| ||{{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|qid=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}||WHS |- |{{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|en|Ichinoyama Kofun|qid=Q11479972|lt=Ichinoyama Kofun|1=Ichinoyama Kofun}}|| 市ノ山古墳（市野山古墳）||keyhole||230m|| ||{{ill|Emperor Ingyō|en|Emperor Ingyō|qid=Q349258|lt=Emperor Ingyō|1=Emperor Ingyō}} ||WHS |- |Hakayama Kofun || 墓山古墳 ||keyhole||225m ||NHS||Emperor Ōjin ''baichō''||WHS |- |Tsudoshiroyama Kofun || 津堂城山古墳 ||keyhole||210m||NHS||Fujiidera Tomb Reference Site||WHS |- |Karusato Ozuka Kofun || 軽里大塚古墳（前の山古墳/白鳥陵古墳）||keyhole||200m||NHS||{{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}||WHS |- |Nonakamiyayama Kofun || 野中宮山古墳||keyhole||154m|| || || |- |Komuroyama Kofun || 古室山古墳||keyhole||150m||NHS|| ||WHS |- |Nonaka Bokeyama Kofun||野中ボケ山古墳||keyhole||122m|| ||{{ill|Emperor Ninken|en|Emperor Ninken|qid=Q357266|lt=Emperor Ninken|1=Emperor Ninken}}|| |- |Takayatsukiyama||高屋築山古墳||keyhole||122m|| ||[[Emperor Ankan]]|| |- |Shirahigeyama Kofun||白髪山古墳||keyhole||115m|| ||{{ill|Emperor Seinei|en|Emperor Seinei|qid=Q357273|lt=Emperor Seinei|1=Emperor Seinei}}|| |- |Otorizuka Kofun||大鳥塚古墳||keyhole||110m||NHS|| ||WHS |- |Futatsuzuka Kofun || 二ツ塚古墳||keyhole||110m|| ||Emperor Ōjin ''baichō''|||WHS |- |Hazamiyama Kofun || はざみ山古墳||keyhole||103m||NHS|| ||WHS |- |Minegazuka Kofun || 峯ヶ塚古墳||keyhole||96m||NHS|| ||WHS |- |Takaya Hachimanyama Kofun || 高屋八幡山古墳||keyhole||90m|| ||Kasuga-no-Yamada himemiko|| |- |Shimaizumi Maruyama Kofun || 島泉丸山古墳]]||circular ||75m|| ||{{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|qid=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}|| |- |Nabezuka Kofun || 鍋塚古墳||square||70m||NHS|| ||WHS |- |Mukohakayama Kofun ||向墓山古墳]]||square||68m|| ||Emperor Ōjin ''baichō''||WHS |- |Jōgenjiyama Kofun||浄元寺山古墳||square||67m||NHS|| ||WHS |- |Aoyama Kofun||青山古墳||round||62m||NHS|| ||WHS |- |Hachizuka Kofun||鉢塚古墳||keyhole||60m||NHS|| ||WHS |- |Karatoyama Kofun||唐櫃山古墳||scallop||59m||NHS|| || |- |Inarizuka Kofun||稲荷塚古墳||scallop||50m||NHS|| || |- |Higashiyama Kofun||東山古墳||square||50m||NHS|| ||WHS |- |Yajimazuka Kofun||八島塚古墳||square||50m|| ||Nakatsu-hime ''baichō''||WHS, one of the "Mitsuzuka Kofun) |- |Nakayamazuka Kofun||中山塚古墳||square||50m|| ||Nakatsu-hime ''baichō''||WHS, one of the "Mitsuzuka Kofun) |- |Shimaizumihirazuka Kofun||島泉平塚古墳]]||square||50m|| ||{{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|qid=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|1=Emperor Yūryaku}} || |- |Kondamaruyama Kofun||誉田丸山古墳||circular||50m|| ||Emperor Ōjin ''baichō''||WHS |- |Koshirahigeyama Kofun||小白髪山古墳||keyhole||46m|| ||Emperor Seinen ''baichō''|| |- |Nishi Umazuka Kofun||西馬塚古墳||square||45m|| ||Emperor Ōjin ''baichō''||WHS |- |Awazuka Kofun||栗塚古墳||square||43m|| ||Emperor Ōjin ''baichō''||WHS |- |Miya-no-minamizuka Kofun||宮の南塚古墳||circular||40m|| ||Emperor Ingyō ''baichō''|| |- |Nonaka Kofun||野中古墳||square||37m||NHS|| ||WHS |- |Suketayama Kofun||助太山古墳||square||36m||NHS||Nakatsu-hime ''baichō''||WHS, one of the "Mitsuzuka Kofun |- |Warizuka Kofun||割塚古墳||square||30m||NHS|| || |- |Sandoyama Kofun||サンド山古墳|| ||30m?|| ||Emperor Ōjin ''baichō''|| |- |Higashi Umazuka Kofun || 東馬塚古墳||square||23m|| ||Emperor Ōjin ''baichō''||WHS |- |Banshoyama Kofun||蕃所山古墳||circular||22m||NHS|| || |- |Matsukawazuka Kofun||松川塚古墳||square||20m||NHS|| || |- |Hayatozuka Kofun||隼人塚古墳]]||square||20m|| ||Emperor Yūryaku ''baichō''|| |- |Nonoue Kofun||野々上古墳||square||20m|| ||Emperor Ninken ''baichō''|| |- |Inuizuka Kofun||衣縫塚古墳||circular||20m|| ||Emperor Ingyō ''baichō''|| |- |Sekimenyama Kofun||赤面山古墳||square||15m||NHS|| || |}
The [[:en:Mozu kofungun|Mozu kofungun]] is located about 10 kilometers to the west. It can be said that it was. The Furuichi Kofun group is seven to fifteen minutes on foot from [[:en:Domyoji Station|Domyoji Station]] or [[:en:Furuichi Station (Ōsaka)|Furuichi Station]] on the {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|qid=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== * [[Kofun]] * [[:en:Mozu kofungun|Mozu kofungun]] *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]] * {{ill|List of National Treasures of Japan (archaeological materials)|en|List of National Treasures of Japan (archaeological materials)|qid=Q6591583|lt=List of National Treasures of Japan (archaeological materials)|1=List of National Treasures of Japan (archaeological materials)}} * [[Cultural Properties of Japan#Buried Cultural Properties|Buried Cultural Properties]] * [[:en:World Heritage Sites in Japan|World Heritage Sites in Japan]]
